title: Fragility Beneath Apparent Stability
summary:
Main
By Michalis Voutsinas
Oil markets began the week on edge as fears mounted over a potential closure of the Strait of Hormuz following U.S. Brent crude futures briefly spiked to $81.40 per barrel on Monday, a five-month high, as geopolitical risk premiums surged in anticipation of Iranian retaliation. However, the market reaction quickly reversed.
